name: Constitution of India & the Preamble

sourceRefs.label: 2017_q1 page 44-45 Q186, Q188 (Secularism added 1976/recognized 1973; French Revolution inspiration, with answer key)
2025_q page 49 Q51 (LIC of India case on Preamble's integral status, with answer key)
standard Preamble facts (dates, exact wording, 42nd Amendment changes) — well-established, no direct past-paper hit needed

researchNotes: Opening topic of Part A Unit IV (Indian Polity) — the largest General Studies unit (15 questions), decomposed into ~12 fine-grained topics per the original content plan. 3 real hits, all clustering on the Preamble's amendment history specifically — a useful signal that TNPSC tests the Preamble's CHANGES (what was added, when, by which case/amendment) more than its plain wording. Expanded 2026-07 pass: added the Objectives Resolution (the Preamble's direct precursor), the Berubari Union case (1960, which had held the Preamble was NOT part of the Constitution) and how Kesavananda Bharati (1973) overruled it — directly resolving the ambiguity r05 had flagged between Kesavananda and the LIC of India case — plus the Preamble's two famous nicknames (Munshi's 'Political Horoscope', Palkhivala's 'Identity Card') and its non-justiciable status.

reading.intro: First of ~12 Unit IV topics. Real exam evidence shows TNPSC favors the Preamble's AMENDMENT history — which words were added when, and which Supreme Court cases established key doctrines — over simply reciting the Preamble's text.

qa.question: When was the Constitution of India adopted, and when did it come into force?
qa.answer: Adopted 26 November 1949 (celebrated as Constitution Day / Samvidhan Divas); came into force 26 January 1950 (celebrated as Republic Day).

qa.question: What are the exact descriptive words in the Preamble describing India, and what is the source of the ideals of Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity?
qa.answer: India is described as a SOVEREIGN, SOCIALIST, SECULAR, DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC. The ideals of Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity embodied in the Preamble were adopted under the inspiration of the French Revolution (confirmed via a real TNPSC question).

qa.question: Real exam: When did the Supreme Court first hold 'Secularism' to be a basic feature of the Constitution, and when was the word actually inserted into the Preamble's text?
qa.answer: The Supreme Court held secularism to be a basic feature in 1973 (via the Kesavananda Bharati case, which established the wider 'Basic Structure' doctrine). The word 'SECULAR' was then formally inserted into the Preamble's text later, by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976.

qa.question: What other words did the 42nd Amendment (1976) add to the Preamble, besides 'Secular'?
qa.answer: 'SOCIALIST' and 'INTEGRITY' (making 'Unity and Integrity of the Nation') were also added by the same 42nd Amendment — none of these three words were in the ORIGINAL 1949 Preamble.

qa.question: Real exam: In which case did the Supreme Court hold that 'the Preamble is an integral part of the Indian Constitution'?
qa.answer: The LIC of India case (1995) — per a real TNPSC exam question (note: the Kesavananda Bharati case, 1973, is the more famous/foundational ruling establishing that the Preamble is part of the Constitution and can itself be amended, overruling the earlier Berubari Union case of 1960 which had said otherwise; the LIC case appears to reaffirm this in a real exam's specific framing).

qa.question: What are the THREE dimensions of 'Justice' named in the Preamble?
What does "WE, THE PEOPLE OF INDIA" at the start of the Preamble signify?
qa.answer: That the Constitution derives its authority directly from the people of India, not from any external authority (like the British Crown) or from the states — it establishes the people as the ultimate source of sovereignty.

qa.question: What was the Objectives Resolution, and how does it relate to the Preamble?
qa.answer: Moved by Jawaharlal Nehru on 13 December 1946 in the Constituent Assembly and unanimously adopted on 22 January 1947, it set out the founding vision and values that directly shaped the Preamble's later wording and the Constitution's overall framework — effectively the Preamble's draft precursor.

qa.question: What did the Berubari Union case (1960) hold about the Preamble's legal status?
qa.answer: The Supreme Court held the Preamble was NOT part of the Constitution and could not be a source of substantive legal power, though it could still serve as a 'key' to understanding the framers' intent.

qa.question: How did the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) change the Berubari position?
qa.answer: It overruled Berubari, holding that the Preamble IS part of the Constitution and CAN be amended under Article 368 — but crucially, no amendment (including to the Preamble) can alter the Constitution's 'Basic Structure'. This is the foundational ruling on the Preamble's status.
qa.tip: Resolves the ambiguity between sources: Kesavananda Bharati (1973) is the doctrinal ORIGIN of 'Preamble = part of the Constitution'; later cases like LIC of India (1995) reaffirm/apply the same principle in a different context, but Kesavananda is the one to cite as foundational.

qa.question: What are the Preamble's two famous descriptive nicknames, and who coined them?
qa.answer: K.M. Munshi called it the 'Political Horoscope' of the Constitution; N.A. Palkhivala famously called it the Constitution's 'Identity Card'.

qa.question: Is the Preamble directly enforceable in a court of law?
qa.answer: No — it is not justiciable (you cannot sue based solely on the Preamble's text), but courts routinely use it as an interpretive aid to resolve ambiguities in the Constitution's other substantive provisions.

questions.prompt: The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into force on:
The ideals of Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity in the Preamble were adopted under the inspiration of:
The Supreme Court first held 'Secularism' to be a basic feature of the Constitution in which year?
The words 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' were added to the Preamble by which amendment?
In which case did the Supreme Court hold that 'the Preamble is an integral part of the Indian Constitution'?
The Preamble names three dimensions of 'Justice'. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: The three are Social, Economic, and Political — not Religious.

questions.prompt: Was the word 'Socialist' present in the Preamble's ORIGINAL 1949 text?
questions.explanation: No — added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.prompt: The Objectives Resolution, the Preamble's founding precursor, was moved by Nehru on:
The Berubari Union case (1960) held that the Preamble:
options: Was NOT part of the Constitution
Could never be discussed in court
questions.explanation: It was NOT part of the Constitution, per this now-overruled 1960 ruling.

questions.prompt: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established that the Preamble:
options: Cannot be amended under any circumstances
Is part of the Constitution and amendable, subject to the Basic Structure doctrine
Is not legally significant at all
questions.explanation: Part of the Constitution, amendable subject to Basic Structure.
questions.trick: Kesavananda Bharati (1973) is the doctrinal origin for 'Preamble = part of Constitution' — cite this case, not just the later LIC of India case, when asked which ruling FIRST established it.

questions.prompt: K.M. Munshi described the Preamble as the:
N.A. Palkhivala famously described the Preamble as the Constitution's:
Is the Preamble directly enforceable (justiciable) in a court of law?
options: Yes, fully enforceable like a Fundamental Right
No, but it is used as an interpretive aid
Yes, but only in the Supreme Court
No, it has no legal relevance whatsoever
questions.explanation: Not justiciable, but used as an interpretive aid.

questions.prompt: Which case FIRST established that the Preamble is part of the Constitution, overruling an earlier contrary view?
The Objectives Resolution was unanimously adopted by the Constituent Assembly on:
